comments.author: @hec9869
comments.text:
ПУТИНСКАЯ РОССИЯ ПРОДОЛЖАЕТ РАЗБАЗАРИВАТЬ РОССИЙСКИЕ ТЕРРИТОРИИ. 

1. ВО ВСЕХ 11 ГУБЕРНИЯХ КАВКАЗА НА МОМЕНТ ВСЕРОССИЙСКОЙ ПЕРЕПИСИ 1897 ГОДА ПРОЖИВАЛО ОКОЛО 8.9 МЛН ЖИТЕЛЕЙ, АРМЯН БЫЛО 12% , Т.Е. БОЛЬШЕ 1 МЛН. ЧЕЛОВЕК. ВСЕ ПОГОЛОВНО АРМЯНЕ БЫЛИ ПЕРЕСЕЛЕНЫ ИЗ ПЕРСИИ В РАЙОН ЕРЕВАНСКОГО НЕ КНЯЖЕСТВА. А ХАНСТВА, И ИЗ ОСМАНСКОЙ ТУРЦИИ ОТ КАРСА ДО ЭРЗЕРУМА В РАЙОН АЛЕКСАНДРАПОЛЯ. 

2. НИКАКОГО ГЮМРИ ДО 1991 В ПРИРОДЕ НЕ СУЩЕСТВОВАЛО. В НАЧАЛЕ НА ЭТОМ МЕСТЕ БЫЛ ГУМРУК - ПОСТ ТУРЕЦКОЙ ТАМОЖНИ НА ГРАНИЦЕ ТУРЦИИ С ПЕРСИЕЙ, ПОСЛЕ ВЗЯТИЯ ТУРЕЦКОГО КАРСА ПАСКЕВИЧЕМ, РУССКИЕ ВОЕННЫЕ СТРОИТЕЛИ И ИНЖЕНЕРЫ ПОСТРОИЛИ РУССКИЙ ГОРОД-КРЕПОСТЬ НА ГРАНИЦЕ С ТУРЦИЕЙ, НАЗВАННЫЙ В 1837 ГОДУ АЛЕКСАНДРАПОЛЕМ, КОТОРЫЙ ВОШЕЛ В СОСТАВ ЛЕНИНЫМ СОЗДАННОЙ АРМССР В 1920 ГОДУ. С 1924 ПЕРЕИМЕНОВАН В ЛЕНИНАКАН. КАК СЕГОДНЯ НАЗЫВАЮТСЯ УЛИЦЫ ГЮМРИ, КОТОРЫЕ НАЗЫВАЛИСЬ ДО 1917: ГЕНЕРАЛЬСКАЯ, НИКОЛАЕВСКАЯ, АЛЕКСАНДРОВСКАЯ, ЛОРИС-МЕЛИКОВСКАЯ, ЕЩЕ ПЯТОК МОГУ НАЗВАТЬ, КАК ЗВАЛИ ПЕРВОГО, КТО ИХ НАЗВАЛ В РУССКОМ АЛЕКСАНДРАПОЛЕ? НИ ОДИН АРМЯНИН И АРМЯНСКИЙ ИСТОРИК НЕ ОТВЕТИТ. ВСЕ РУСТОВАННЫЕ ДОМА С ПРЯМЫМИ УЛИЦАМИ ПОСТРОЕНЫ РУССКИМИ ВОЕННЫМИ СТРОИТЕЛЯМИ, АРМЯНЕ ЖИЛИ В ПОЛУПОДЗЕМНЫХ САКЛЯХ С ПЛОСКИМИ КРЫШАМИ В ДЗОРИ-МАЙЛА, БОШИ-МАЙЛА, ТУРКИ-МАЙЛА И ПРОЧИМИ МАЙЛА-МАХАЛЛЯ НА АЗИЯТСКИЦ МАНЕР С КРИВЫМИ УЛИЦАМИ ПО КОТОРЫ ТЕКЛИ ИСПРАЖНЕНИЯ. 

3. ЕРЕВАН - БЫВШИЙ АЗИАТСКИЙ КИШЛАК ПЕРЕХОДИЛ ОТ ПЕРСОВ К ТУРКАМ И НАЗАД ДЕСЯТКИ РАЗ. ИЗВЕСТНЫ ПОИМЕННО ТУРЕЦКИЕ И ПЕРСИДСКИЕ ХАНЫ КАК МИНИМУМ С 1300 ГОДА, АРМЯНЕ БЫЛИ ИНОРОДЦАМИ. ВОЗРАСТ КИШЛАКА ЕРЕВАН ОКОЛО 1600 ЛЕТ, ПОСЛЕ ВЗЯТИЯ ПАСКЕВИЧЕМ ТЕГЕРАНА В 1828 КИШЛАК ВОШЕЛ В СОСТАВ РОССИЙСКОЙ ИМПЕРИИ. 

4. ВСЯ ПРОМЫШЛЕННОСТЬ АРМССР БЫЛА СОЗДАНА РОССИЕЙ, ГОСУДАРСТВЕННЫЕ ГРАНИЦЫ ОХРАНЯЛИСЬ СОВЕТСКОЙ АРМИЕЙ, КОРРУПЦИЯ ЗАШКАЛИВАЛА С ПЕРВЫХ ДНЕЙ СУЩЕСТВОВАНИЯ, АРМССР ЖИЛА ЗА СЧЕТ ДОТАЦИЙ В ДЕСЯТКИ РАЗ ЛУЧШЕ, ЧЕМ ЛЮБОЙ РАЙОН РСФСР. 

5. СЕГОДНЯ ЖИТЕЛЕЙ В САМОЙ АРМЕНИИ В ДВА РАЗА МЕНЬШЕ, ЧЕМ АРМЯН ЗАХВАТИВШИХ ТЕРРИТОРИИ РОССИЙСКОЙ ФЕДЕРАЦИИ И УСТАНОВИВШИХ НА НИХ ВЛАСТЬ НЕ ПОДВЛАСТНУЮ КРЕМЛЮ. АРМЯНСКИМ ОПГ НА ТЕРРИТОРИИ РОССИИ ПЛЕВАТЬ ПОД КОГО ЛЯЖЕТ ПАШИНЯН И ЕГО СВОРА, ХОТЬ ПОД ТУРКОВ, ХОТЬ ПОД АРАБСКОГО ЦАРЬКА, ЛИШЬ БЫ НА ХАЛЯВУ.

comments.author: @mariadespina80
comments.text:
Romania, the ancient 2000-year-old Kingdom of Dacia, was Christianized by The Apostles Andrew and Philip who brought and preached the Bible in Dacia-Romania  in the Ist century AD. . 
The Bishopric of Tomis, the current city of Constanta (Scythia Minor - the Dobrogea region today):    Although located in Dobrogea, this was the oldest organized episcopal structure in the region, with bishops known since the 3rd-4th centuries (e.g. Betranion).
Ruins of basilicas and cult objects have been found at Sucidava, a Dacian fortress, and Morisena, which attest to the existence of organized church structures (possibly bishops or missionary bishops/horepiscops) on the territory of Dacia. In conclusion, the episcopal organization began to take shape in the 4th-6th centuries, being closely linked to the Roman administrative organization and the missionary activity of the bishops in the neighboring provinces  of the Danube.  The first bishopric
The Bishopric of Tomis was the first bishopric established on the territory of our country Romania today.
The Holy Apostle Philip accompanied the Holy Apostle Andrew to today's Dobrogea, to preach the word of God to the people here. The oldest source that mentions them is the Gothic Calendar.
According to the historian H. Achelis, the Gothic Calendar was compiled in Thrace, where Emperor Theodosius I (379-395) granted asylum to the Goths who had fled north of the Danube, from the Huns, in the year 380.
This Calendar introduced holidays specific to the Arian Goths, as well as Christian holidays.
The Gothic Calendar included among the feast days specific to Christians in this area the Holy Apostle Andrew and the Holy Apostle Philip, as preachers of the Gospel in Scythia Minor, today's Dobrogea.
The deeds of the two missionaries are also found in other later documents, such as the apocryphal Writing of Obadiah and the Martyrology of Adon, the latter composed between 855-860.
The historian Sozomen (6th century) recalls that Scythia Minor as a whole came under the rule of a single bishop who had his residence in the metropolis of the province, Tomis, the current city of Constanta, on the shores of the Black Sea-Romania

replies.author: @margaretenglish4326
replies.text: ​@mariadespina80 Thank you for your update. We learn something new everyday. God bless

replies.author: @mariadespina80
replies.text:
@margaretenglish4326 The split between the Primary Church (undivided) and what we know today as the Catholic Church (and the Orthodox Church) officially occurred in the year \(1054\). Romania has the Apostolic Orthodox faith.
The word "orthodox" comes from the Greek language, from the union of two words: orthos = right, correct; doxa = belief, opinion, glory. Traditional from the beginning.  ,  Romania has the Orthodox Apostolic faith.

replies.author: @margaretenglish4326
replies.text: Most interesting piece of Christian (Catholic) history. Thank you so much for sharing your knowledge. God bless you

comments.author: @mariadespina80
comments.text:
Romania, the ancient 2000-year-old Kingdom of Dacia, was Christianized by The Apostles Andrew and Philip who brought and preached the Bible in Dacia-Romania  in the Ist century AD. . 
The beginnings of Christianity on the territory of present-day Romania (former Dacia and the Lower Danube area) are closely linked to the martyrdom of many believers, especially in the 3rd-4th centuries AD, during the Roman persecutions. The Christian martyrs of this period are some of the earliest heralds of the faith, suffering martyrdom especially during the persecutions of the emperors Diocletian (303-304) or Galerius. The best-known Christian martyrs of the first centuries: Martyrs of Dobrogea (Scythia Minor): This region provided the most known martyrs from the early period. Saint Emilian of Durostorum (Silistra): Martyred in 362 AD. Saint Dasius of Durostorum (Silistra): Roman soldier martyred in the early 4th century (ca. 304 AD) for refusing to participate in pagan celebrations. Saint Julius the Veteran (from Durostorum): Also a soldier who confessed his faith before the Roman authorities. Martyrs of Niculițel (Zotikos, Attalos, Kamasis and Filippos): Their remains were discovered in 1971, in a crypt in Niculițel, Tulcea County in Romania dating from the 4th century. Epictetus and Astion: Two of the oldest martyrs, who suffered at Halmyris (near the current locality of Murighiol in Tulcea - Romania) in 290 AD.
Historical context of the martyrdom:Period: The martyrdoms took place mainly in the 3rd and 4th centuries, a period in which Christianity was spreading among the Dacians and the Romans, despite imperial prohibitions.Location: Most archaeological and literary testimonies about the martyrs come from the Lower Danube area (Dobrogea - Romania), which was under stricter Roman administration. Christians refused to worship the Roman gods or make sacrifices to the emperor, preferring death to preserve their faith in Jesus Christ. These martyrs are venerated by the Romanian Orthodox Church as examples of faith and steadfastness, testifying to the continuity of Christianity on these lands.
